title: RNS_Epilepsy-iBIDS
description: A long-term intracranial electroencephalography (iEEG) dataset from 8 epilepsy patients implanted with responsive neurostimulation (RNS) devices, comprising bilateral recordings from epileptic lesion areas. Each recording captures 90 seconds of dual-channel iEEG centered on stimulation events, with detailed stimulation parameters and seizure event markers. The dataset enables investigation of seizure onset pattern evolution and iEEG characteristic changes following electrical stimulation therapy.
methods_description: iEEG data were recorded invasively from bilateral epileptic lesion areas in RNS-implanted patients. Each recording contains 90 seconds of dual-channel iEEG (60 seconds pre-stimulation and ~30 seconds post-stimulation). Data were acquired in weekly sessions over periods exceeding six months per patient. Stimulation parameters were documented by neurosurgeons during clinical follow-ups. Data were saved in EDF format and converted to BIDS using FieldTrip (version 20250114). Technical validation included power spectral analysis, envelope length calculation, and phase locking value computation.

funding_references.funder_name: National Science and Technology Major Project
National Natural Science Foundation of China
